title: Cyanea longiflora
taxonomy: Cyanea longiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Hawaii, specifically found on the island of Kauai. The plant is a member of the genus Cyanea, which is part of the larger family Goodeniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the goodenia family or the sutured goodenia family. The classification of Cyanea longiflora places it as a distinct species within the Goodeniaceae family.
uses: Cyanea longiflora, a rare and endangered plant, has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Cyanea longiflora is used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. However, further scientific research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. The plant's medicinal uses are still being studied and documented by modern botanists and researchers.

intro: Cyanea longiflora, also known as the Hawaiian sky flower or long-flowered cyanea, is a flowering plant species native to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide relief from pain and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyanea longiflora include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures, to support health and well-being.
